mnemonics.title: Thread States Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: New Threads Run By Eating Pizza - N(ew), R(eady), R(unning), B(locked), E(rminated)

mnemonics.title: Deadlock Conditions
mnemonics.content: Circular Wait Holds No Preemption - Circular wait, Hold and wait, No preemption, Mutual exclusion (first letter of each forms CNHP)

mnemonics.title: Multithreading Benefits
mnemonics.content: PRIS - Parallelism, Responsiveness, Resource sharing, Improved throughput

acronyms.usage: Standard thread API for Unix/Linux systems providing thread creation, management, and synchronization functions
Threads managed entirely by user-space libraries without kernel involvement
Threads directly managed by the operating system kernel
Synchronization primitive that ensures only one thread accesses a shared resource at a time
Memory architecture where memory access time depends on memory location relative to the processor

visualTips.visualization: Draw a process as a rectangle with code/data/heap segments, and threads as smaller rectangles inside it sharing those segments but each having own stack
Two arrows (threads) racing to a finish line (shared resource) with unpredictable winner causing inconsistent results
Two people holding one fork each, each waiting for the other's fork - circular wait shown as cycle in diagram
Draw a queue of tasks feeding into a pool of fixed worker threads that process them repeatedly

analogies.analogy: Like employees in an office sharing the same printer and files but each having their own desk (stack) and task list (program counter)
Like a single bathroom key - only one person can enter at a time, others must wait outside until key is returned
Like a parking lot with limited spaces - count decreases when car enters, increases when car leaves, no entry when full
Like a restaurant pager - you wait (block) until your number is called (signal) indicating your table is ready
